title: snurb.info
description: This page from snurb.info aggregates mentions and news related to Fabio Giglietto's academic work, including recent press reviews and announcements concerning his research on disinformation, social media, and European elections.

title: ilrestodelcarlino.it
description: The article reports on a research project coordinated by Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino, investigating how social media algorithms influence consensus and facilitate the spread of manipulative content, directly aligning with his expertise in disinformation and social media analysis.

title: XIX Legislatura - Lavori - Progetti di legge - Camera.it (referring to 'Resoconti Assemblea')
snippet: Fabio Giglietto's study, titled "Meta's Political content reduction policy and Italian parliamentarians' Facebook visibility," which analyzed 2.5 million Facebook posts, was cited in a parliamentary session of the Italian Chamber of Deputies on February 27, 2026, within discussions on digital platform transparency.
description: A parliamentary mention on camera.it citing Fabio Giglietto's study on Meta's political content policy and Facebook visibility in Italy during discussions on digital platform transparency, scheduled for February 27, 2026.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article discusses a study published on January 7, 2026, by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino. The research found that Facebook's algorithm suppresses official political content while boosting extremist and anti-European narratives, leading to calls for bipartisan legislation on platforms.
description: A news article from open.online discusses a study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, published on January 7, 2026, finding that Facebook's algorithm suppresses official political content while boosting extremist narratives.
